data.text: I’ve confirmed the current federation package only has fan-out. Next I’m checking whether project ownership already exists somewhere else in server state or GraphQL, because the new routing helper needs a real source of truth rather than inventing a new map.

data.text: I still need the ownership rule, so I’m checking the existing federation e2e scenario and the server’s GraphQL federation model to see how the project-to-spoke mapping is expected to be derived in practice.

data.output:
paletteSearch(
    """Search query"""
    query: String!
  ): PaletteSearchResults!

  # ==========================================
  # Federation Queries (B14.6b)
  # ==========================================
  # The hub federates read-only queries across registered spokes via the
  # B14.6a fan-out client. Local query types (beads, runs, projects) remain
  # untouched — these federated queries ship in parallel so existing
  # local views do not change. Each federated row carries routing metadata
  # (node_id, project_id, project_url, write_capability/status) so the UI
  # can deep-link back to the originating spoke and reason about whether
  # it may issue writes (currently always read-only).

  """List all spokes registered with this hub, including their routing metadata and last-known liveness status. Returns an empty list when the server is not running in hub mode."""
  federationNodes: [FederationNode!]!

  """List beads aggregated across the federation. Fan-out is read-only; each row exposes the originating spoke's routing metadata."""
  federatedBeads(
    """Filter by lifecycle status"""
    status: String,
    """Filter by label"""
    label: String,
    """Filter by project ID (applied per-spoke)"""
    projectID: String
  ): [FederatedBead!]!

  """List runs aggregated across the federation."""
  federatedRuns(
    """Filter by run layer"""
    layer: RunLayer,
    """Filter by project ID (applied per-spoke)"""
    projectID: String
  ): [FederatedRun!]!

  """List projects aggregated across the federation."""
  federatedProjects(
    """Include projects whose path is unreachable on the spoke"""
    includeUnreachable: Boolean
  ): [FederatedProject!]!

  # ==========================================
  # Agent Metrics (Story 11)
  # ==========================================

  """Return windowed agent execution metrics grouped by the requested axis.
  Backed by FEAT-010 run-store with .ddx/executions bundle fallback. Cache
  is keyed by run-store revision so it survives ADR-004 archive moves —
  callers can read revision off the response and re-issue when it changes."""
  agentMetrics(
    """Time window to aggregate over. Default 7d (Story 11 locked: all-time removed)."""
    window: AgentMetricsWindow! = W7D,
    """Grouping axis: MODEL, HARNESS, PROVIDER, or TIER (harness/model)."""
    groupBy: AgentMetricsAxis!
  ): AgentMetricsResult!
}

# ============================================
# Agent Metrics (Story 11)
# ============================================

"""AgentMetricsWindow enumerates the time windows the agentMetrics resolver supports. The Story 11 locked decision removed all-time from the UI; only 24h, 7d, and 30d are exposed."""
enum AgentMetricsWindow {
  """Trailing 24 hours"""
  W24H
  """Trailing 7 days (default)"""
  W7D
  """Trailing 30 days"""
  W30D
}

"""AgentMetricsAxis selects the grouping dimension for agentMetrics rows."""
enum AgentMetricsAxis {
  """Group by model identifier"""
  MODEL
  """Group by harness name"""
  HARNESS
  """Group by provider name"""
  PROVIDER
  """Group by harness/model tier (TierKey)"""
  TIER
}

"""AgentMetricsRow is one aggregated bucket: counts, success rate, duration percentiles, and cost/token means for the attempts in `key`."""
type AgentMetricsRow {
  """Group key (model, harness, provider, or tier label)"""
  key: String!
  """Total attempts in this bucket"""
  attempts: Int!
  """Successful attempts (success + already_satisfied per Story 11)"""
  successes: Int!
  """successes / attempts"""
  successRate: Float!
  """Mean duration in milliseconds"""
  meanDurationMs: Float!
  """Median (p50) duration in milliseconds"""
  p50DurationMs: Int!
  """95th-percentile duration in milliseconds"""
  p95DurationMs: Int!
  """Mean cost in USD"""
  meanCostUsd: Float!
  """Effective cost per success: total cost / successes (null when no successes)"""
  effectiveCostPerSuccessUsd: Float
  """Mean input tokens (only run-store records carry tokens)"""
  meanInputTokens: Float!
  """Mean output tokens"""
  meanOutputTokens: Float!
  """Most recent attempt finish time in this bucket"""
  lastSeenAt: DateTime
}

"""AgentMetricsResult wraps the row set with the window/axis echo plus a revision string. The revision is a fingerprint of the run-store + bundle + archive inputs; callers may use it as a cache key."""
type AgentMetricsResult {
  """Echo of the requested window"""
  window: AgentMetricsWindow!
  """Echo of the requested grouping axis"""
  groupBy: AgentMetricsAxis!
  """Revision token for the underlying data sources; opaque cache key."""
  revision: String!
  """Aggregated rows, sorted by attempt count descending then key ascending."""
  rows: [AgentMetricsRow!]!
}

# ============================================
# Federation (B14.6b)
# ============================================

"""FederationNode is one entry in the hub's federation registry, decorated with the most recent fan-out outcome so callers can render partial-result and version-skew states."""
type FederationNode {
  """Globally unique identifier (uses the spoke's node_id)"""
  id: ID!
  """Stable federation key for the spoke"""
  nodeId: String!
  """Human-readable spoke name"""
  name: String!
  """Spoke base URL (used as the deep-link target for federated rows)"""
  url: String!
  """Liveness/health status: registered, active, stale, offline, degraded"""
  status: String!
  """ddx_version advertised by the spoke at registration"""
  ddxVersion: String!
  """schema_version advertised by the spoke at registration"""
  schemaVersion: String!
  """Capabilities advertised by the spoke (synthetic @identity:* entries are filtered out)"""
  capabilities: [String!]!
  """When the spoke was first registered with the hub"""
  registeredAt: DateTime!
  """Last observed heartbeat timestamp (null if never seen)"""
  lastHeartbeat: DateTime
  """True when the spoke advertises a write capability. Currently always false — federated queries are read-only."""
  writeCapability: Boolean!
  """Reason text for the last skip/failure outcome (e.g. version-skew handshake reason). Empty when the spoke contributed successfully."""
  lastError: String
}

"""FederatedBead is a bead row aggregated across spokes; the routing metadata identifies which spoke the bead lives on."""
type FederatedBead {
  """Spoke node_id this row originated from"""
  nodeId: String!
  """Project ID on the originating spoke (may be null when the spoke does not partition by project)"""
  projectId: String
  """Spoke base URL — the UI deep-links here for write/edit"""
  projectUrl: String!
  """True when the originating spoke advertises a write capability"""
  writeCapability: Boolean!
  """Liveness/health status of the originating spoke at fan-out time"""
  status: String!
  """The bead row itself"""
  bead: Bead!
}

"""FederatedRun is a run row aggregated across spokes."""
type FederatedRun {
  """Spoke node_id this row originated from"""
  nodeId: String!
  """Project ID on the originating spoke (when known)"""
  projectId: String
  """Spoke base URL"""
  projectUrl: String!
  """True when the originating spoke advertises a write capability"""
  writeCapability: Boolean!
  """Liveness/health status of the originating spoke at fan-out time"""
  status: String!
  """The run row itself"""
  run: Run!
}

"""FederatedProject is a project row aggregated across spokes."""
type FederatedProject {
  """Spoke node_id this row originated from"""
  nodeId: String!
  """Project ID on the originating spoke"""
  projectId: String!
  """Spoke base URL"""
  projectUrl: String!
  """True when the originating spoke advertises a write capability"""
  writeCapability: Boolean!
  """Liveness/health status of the originating spoke at fan-out time"""
  status: String!
  """The project row itself"""
  project: Project!
}

# ============================================
# Mutations
# ============================================

data.text: The repository doesn’t yet encode project ownership in the registry, so I’m checking the bead lineage and tracker metadata before I choose a minimal implementation path. That should tell me whether ownership is supposed to be inferred from existing state or added here.

ID:       ddx-eb75a32d
Title:    S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation)
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, story:15, kind:multi-node, area:federation
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-256af8b5
Deps:     ddx-256af8b5
Desc:     PROBLEM
Federation today supports query fan-out only (cli/internal/federation/fanout.go). For the multi-node operator-prompt path, the coordinator (hub) must route a mutation to a single spoke that owns the project, NOT fan out. No mutation routing envelope, no project-based spoke selection, and no forwarding client exist.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: FanOut function sends read queries to all spokes; has no concept of routing to a single owning spoke for mutations.
- FederationRegistry (from ddx-90f48012): stores spoke records by node_id but no lookup-by-project-id helper exists.
- No HTTP client method ForwardMutation exists in the federation package.
- Plan-locked decision (point 6 from story-15 planning): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations; mutation envelope is HTTP POST to spoke /graphql with X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity headers preserved verbatim.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry FederationRegistry, projectID string) (*SpokeRecord, error): looks up the spoke that owns the project; returns error when no owner registered.
- Add federation client method ForwardMutation(ctx context.Context, target *SpokeRecord, body []byte, headers map[string]string) (*http.Response, error): issues HTTP POST to <spoke>/graphql with supplied headers preserved verbatim.
- Add hub HTTP handler that accepts forwarding requests and routes by project (the handler that S15-7c and S15-7d build on top of).
- Identity header values are NOT populated in this bead (that is S15-7c / ddx-ba31fec8); this bead lays the envelope and routing.

NON-SCOPE
- Dual authorization on the spoke side (ddx-ba31fec8).
- Identity attestation structure (ddx-e2c217d1).
- Changing existing query fan-out behavior.
Accept:   1. RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry, projectID) returns correct SpokeRecord or error when no owner registered.
2. ForwardMutation(ctx, target, body, headers) issues POST to <spoke>/graphql with supplied headers preserved verbatim.
3. Hub handler that accepts mutation forwarding requests exists and routes by project.
4. TestRouteMutation_CorrectSpoke verifies routing to the correct spoke.
5. TestRouteMutation_NoOwner_ReturnsError verifies error when project owner unknown.
6. TestForwardMutation_HeadersReachSpoke verifies headers arrive at spoke unchanged.
7. No behavior change to existing query fan-out tests.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

ID:       ddx-256af8b5
Title:    S15-7: Multi-node trust attestation forwarding (Story-14 coupling)
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:15, kind:multi-node, blocking, reconciled:no-changes-state
Parent:   ddx-1d85c927
Deps:     ddx-a7e7a1a1
Desc:     PROBLEM
BLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement. In the coordinator topology (Story 14), the originating peer identity attestation is not forwarded from coordinator to client — any non-localhost write goes through the hub without carrying who initiated it. The spoke cannot authorize on both origin and coordinator identity because neither is transmitted.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: current federation supports query fan-out only. No RouteMutationToProjectOwner, no ForwardMutation, no identity header propagation.
- Risk ('Multi-node authorization gap'): A compromised or misconfigured hub can forward mutations on behalf of any origin, with the spoke unable to distinguish legitimate coordinator-forwarded requests from hub-originated attacks. Without dual authorization, spoke trusts the hub implicitly.
- The /tmp/story-15-final.md plan file cited in the original bead is ephemeral and not available to sub-agents on other machines; relevant content is inlined below.

PLAN-LOCKED DECISIONS (from story-15 planning):
- Multi-node routing (point 6): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations.
- Identity forwarding: X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified WhoIs peer at coordinator edge (NOT coordinator self); X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Both mandatory on forwarded mutations.
- Dual authorization on spoke: requireTrusted augmented to check BOTH origin (WhoIs / loopback policy) AND coordinator (registered federation hub); 403 if either fails.

This bead has been decomposed into 4 children:
- ddx-468d800f — S15-7a: ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers.
- ddx-eb75a32d — S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation).
- ddx-ba31fec8 — S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization.
- ddx-ea1ada47 — S15-7d: Integration test with two in-process servers.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT spec; changes go into FEAT-010 and FEAT-011 extensions.
- Performance optimization of federation routing.
- Story 14 query fan-out behavior (already shipped in fanout.go).
Accept:   1. Coordinator forwards origin identity (X-DDx-Origin-Identity) unchanged in mutation envelope from ddx-eb75a32d.
2. Client-side authorization (ddx-ba31fec8) checks both origin and coordinator identity; returns 403 when either missing or untrusted.
3. ADR-conformance lint (ddx-468d800f) added to CI and fails on any new mutation resolver missing requireTrusted or CSRF/Origin checks.
4. TestRequireTrusted_ForwardedMutation_AuthorizesOriginAndCoordinator passes (happy path).
5. TestFederation_OperatorPromptSubmit_CoordinatorToSpoke_IdentityUnchanged (ddx-ea1ada47) exercises full stack.
6. Three negative sub-tests (untrusted-coordinator, missing-origin, tampered-origin) all return 403.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

ROOT CAUSE

- Risk ('Multi-node authorization gap'): A compromised or misconfigured hub can forward mutations on behalf of any origin, with the spoke unable to distinguish legitimate coordinator-forwarded requests from hub-originated attacks. Without dual authorization, spoke trusts the hub implicitly.
- The /tmp/story-15-final.md plan file cited in the original bead is ephemeral and not available to sub-agents on other machines; relevant content is inlined below.

PLAN-LOCKED DECISIONS (from story-15 planning):

- Identity forwarding: X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified WhoIs peer at coordinator edge (NOT coordinator self); X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Both mandatory on forwarded mutations.
- Dual authorization on spoke: requireTrusted augmented to check BOTH origin (WhoIs / loopback policy) AND coordinator (registered federation hub); 403 if either fails.

In the coordinator→client mutation path (Story 14/15), the originating peer identity must be forwarded from coordinator to spoke unchanged, and the spoke must authorize on both the origin AND the coordinator identity. Today cli/internal/federation/fanout.go supports only query fan-out with no identity forwarding; the mutation envelope from S15-7b carries headers but no production caller enforces dual authorization on the spoke side.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: query fan-out exists but no requireTrustedForwarded middleware or augmentation of requireTrusted handles forwarded mutations.
- The spoke's requireTrusted (cli/internal/server/server.go) checks only the immediate transport peer — it does not parse X-DDx-Origin-Identity or X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity headers.
- No dual-authorization path exists: a forwarded mutation that passes coordinator trust but carries an untrusted origin is not rejected.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add requireTrustedForwarded middleware (or augment requireTrusted) in cli/internal/server/server.go:
  - Parse X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity from incoming request.
  - Validate origin identity against tailnet WhoIs / loopback policy.
  - Validate coordinator identity against registered federation peers.
  - Return 403 if either check fails or either header is missing.
- Coordinator forwarding path (built in S15-7b): when forwarding a prompt-driven mutation, set X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified incoming peer identity (from WhoIs at coordinator edge); set X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Identity MUST NOT be rewritten in transit.

NON-SCOPE
- Identity signing/attestation structure (that's ddx-e2c217d1).
- Mutation routing envelope (that's S15-7b / ddx-eb75a32d, a dep).
- Non-mutation endpoints.","id":"ddx-ba31fec8","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:15","kind:multi-node","kind:auth","blocking"],"parent":"ddx-256af8b5","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization (origin + coordinator)","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:08:00.1636411Z"}

- Recognized DDx-owned scratch prefixes: `ddx-exec-wt`, `ddx-claim-heartbeats`, `ddx-metric-keepalive`, `ddx-test-`, `ddx-e2e-`, and any temp root containing DDx `cleanup.json` metadata with matching project ownership.
- Minimum age: metadata-backed paths may be removed when liveness is expired or the owning attempt is terminal; metadata-less recognized-prefix scratch roots require mtime age >= 6 hours and no live PID/session evidence.
- Hard floor: preserve the current hard stop floor of 64 MiB free bytes and 1024 free inodes until a config override bead changes it.

- Identity header values are NOT populated in this bead (that is S15-7c / ddx-ba31fec8); this bead lays the envelope and routing.

# Routing-cleanup lint (`routinglint`)

`routinglint` is the CI-enforced dead-code / anti-reintroduction check for
the compensating DDx-side routing helpers retired by **ddx-3bd7396a** under
**FEAT-006** (parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7, cleanup bead ddx-653f6ac9).

## Why it exists

Parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7 said:

> Search DDx for code that re-implements upstream routing concerns:
> allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,
> provider cost scoring. Must be absent on the default path.
> `ResolveProfileLadder`, `ResolveTierModelRef`, `workersByHarness`
> escalation helpers remain reachable via `--escalate` only. Dead-code
> detector run in CI catches orphans.

The follow-up cleanup bead **ddx-3bd7396a** went further than the parent
AC: it deleted those helpers and the `--escalate` / `--override-model`
flags entirely, and made `agent.routing.profile_ladders` /
`agent.routing.model_overrides` config keys hard-error at load. The
"reachable via `--escalate` only" half of the AC is therefore moot — the
default path is the *only* path.

`routinglint` enforces the stricter post-cleanup invariant: those
identifiers and flag/config-key strings must not reappear anywhere in
`cli/` Go source. If they do, the routing cleanup has regressed and CI
fails.

These retired names are not active DDx routing APIs. They may appear only in
lint fixtures, hard-error rejection paths, or migration/deprecation docs.

## What it checks

Two closed lists, both maintained as Go constants in
`cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go`:

- **Forbidden identifiers** (exact `Ident.Name` match — substrings inside
  larger identifiers such as test-function names are not flagged):
  `ResolveProfileLadder`, `ResolveTierModelRef`,
  `ResolveProfileLadderCallCount`, `AdaptiveMinTier`, `workersByHarness`.

- **Forbidden string literals** (exact value match):
  `--escalate`, `--override-model`,
  `profile_ladders`, `model_overrides`,
  `agent.routing.profile_ladders`, `agent.routing.model_overrides`.

A diagnostic is emitted at every occurrence in any package under `cli/`.
The analyzer's own package (`tools/lint/routinglint/...`) is exempt
because the closed-list constants legitimately embed every forbidden
token.

## Where it runs in CI

The lint is wired into the lefthook `ci` block as `routing-lint-all`
(see `lefthook.yml`), which the GitHub Actions `CI Validation` job
invokes via `lefthook run ci`. The full-tree gate runs on every push
and PR — there is no staged-file fast-path because the invariant is
"zero matches anywhere", not "no new matches in the diff".

To run it locally:

```bash
cd cli
go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...
```

Exit code 0 means the cleanup is intact. Any non-zero exit code lists
the offending file/line and which retired symbol/literal was found.

## Deliberate rejection paths

Some code paths *must* mention the retired tokens by literal — for
example, `cli/internal/config/config.go::checkRoutingMigration` reads
`profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` keys out of an incoming config
to issue the hard-error rejection that ddx-3bd7396a §AC#4 mandates;
the corresponding tests in `cli/internal/config/routing_migration_test.go`
and `cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_routing_reject_test.go` assert that the
error message names the retired field.

Those occurrences are exempted by an inline annotation:

```go
// routinglint:legacy-rejection reason="hard-error rejection of retired routing keys per ddx-3bd7396a"
for _, field := range []string{"default_harness", "profile_ladders", "model_overrides"} {
```

The annotation may sit on the same line as the offending node or on a
comment line directly above it. The `reason="..."` clause must be
present and non-empty; the analyzer reports a separate diagnostic
("annotation is missing a non-empty reason= clause") if it is omitted.

The exemption is **per-line**, not per-file or per-package. Adding it
is a deliberate, reviewable signal that this specific occurrence is the
rejection / migration path, not new compensating-routing logic.

## Updating the closed lists

If the cleanup ever needs to retire an additional helper or flag,
extend `forbiddenIdents` or `forbiddenLiterals` in
`cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go` and add a fixture line in
`cli/tools/lint/routinglint/testdata/src/violations/violations.go` so
`TestViolations` pins the new diagnostic.

The analyzer is intentionally conservative: it matches identifiers
exactly and string literals exactly. It does not pattern-match cost
scoring or allow-list shapes structurally — those are caught at code
review against the bullet list above. The closed-list approach keeps
false positives close to zero and makes every regression a one-line
fix or a one-line annotation, with the reviewer in the loop.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T163146-2c15e6f6/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T163146-2c15e6f6/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T163146-2c15e6f6/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T163146-2c15e6f6/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T163146-2c15e6f6/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.
   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-eb75a32d">     <title>S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation)</title>     <description> PROBLEM
Federation today supports query fan-out only (cli/internal/federation/fanout.go). For the multi-node operator-prompt path, the coordinator (hub) must route a mutation to a single spoke that owns the project, NOT fan out. No mutation routing envelope, no project-based spoke selection, and no forwarding client exist.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: FanOut function sends read queries to all spokes; has no concept of routing to a single owning spoke for mutations.
- FederationRegistry (from ddx-90f48012): stores spoke records by node_id but no lookup-by-project-id helper exists.
- No HTTP client method ForwardMutation exists in the federation package.
- Plan-locked decision (point 6 from story-15 planning): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations; mutation envelope is HTTP POST to spoke /graphql with X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity headers preserved verbatim.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry FederationRegistry, projectID string) (*SpokeRecord, error): looks up the spoke that owns the project; returns error when no owner registered.
- Add federation client method ForwardMutation(ctx context.Context, target *SpokeRecord, body []byte, headers map[string]string) (*http.Response, error): issues HTTP POST to <spoke>/graphql with supplied headers preserved verbatim.
- Add hub HTTP handler that accepts forwarding requests and routes by project (the handler that S15-7c and S15-7d build on top of).
- Identity header values are NOT populated in this bead (that is S15-7c / ddx-ba31fec8); this bead lays the envelope and routing.

NON-SCOPE
- Dual authorization on the spoke side (ddx-ba31fec8).
- Identity attestation structure (ddx-e2c217d1).
- Changing existing query fan-out behavior.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry, projectID) returns correct SpokeRecord or error when no owner registered.
2. ForwardMutation(ctx, target, body, headers) issues POST to <spoke>/graphql with supplied headers preserved verbatim.
3. Hub handler that accepts mutation forwarding requests exists and routes by project.
4. TestRouteMutation_CorrectSpoke verifies routing to the correct spoke.
5. TestRouteMutation_NoOwner_ReturnsError verifies error when project owner unknown.
6. TestForwardMutation_HeadersReachSpoke verifies headers arrive at spoke unchanged.
7. No behavior change to existing query fan-out tests.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:2, story:15, kind:multi-node, area:federation</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-256af8b5" base-rev="c5df37a4cdca90668bf90b3956cf4142a093e77a" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T163146-2c15e6f6"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved.
